jokes.text: Being a robot's great, but we don't have emotions... and sometimes that makes me very sad.
jokes.setup: Bender claims robots don't have emotions
jokes.punchline: Immediately expresses sadness about not having emotions
jokes.explanation: Classic robot paradox joke with perfect economy. The contradiction is immediate and character-perfect for Bender.

jokes.text: Oh, Lordy Lou, there he goes again! Well, let's give baby what he wants.
jokes.setup: Bender's emotional contradiction
jokes.punchline: Professor treats the sophisticated robot like a whining child
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent dismissive humor from Professor, treating advanced robot like a child.

jokes.text: I've already tested it by turning the toaster into a raccoon.
jokes.setup: Professor explains reverse fossilization process
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions turning kitchen appliance into wild animal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Professor moment. The casual delivery of something impossible makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kind of gamy.
jokes.setup: Mention of turning toaster into raccoon
jokes.punchline: Understated food critique of what was once an appliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up. The understated food review of ex-toaster raccoon is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: Cover your shame, mon!
jokes.setup: Bender has just transformed into naked human
jokes.punchline: Hermes' Jamaican-accented prudish reaction to nudity
jokes.explanation: Character-perfect reaction from Hermes. The Jamaican accent adds to the comedy of his prudishness.

jokes.text: It worked! Eat it, everyone who's never won a Nobel Prize! And that includes you, Amy.
jokes.setup: Professor celebrates successful experiment
jokes.punchline: Goes from general bragging to specifically insulting Amy
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor ego moment with perfect escalation from general to personal insult.

jokes.text: Nah, it just moved. I'm not getting good reception on it, though.
jokes.setup: Bender notices his antenna is gone as human
jokes.punchline: Reveals it relocated to his groin and comments on poor reception
jokes.explanation: Clever anatomy joke that maintains Bender's robotic perspective on his new human body.

jokes.text: Maybe if I wiggle it around... No! You'll make God cry!
jokes.setup: Bender wants to improve reception on relocated antenna
jokes.punchline: Hermes' horrified religious objection to the implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Hermes' over-the-top religious reaction to Bender's innocent antenna adjustment.

jokes.text: Hooray! It's just like Mardi Gras.
jokes.setup: Bender is presumably dancing or celebrating
jokes.punchline: Professor compares it to a festival known for debauchery and nudity
jokes.explanation: Simple comparison joke. The Mardi Gras reference is predictable but works for the visual.

jokes.text: Come into the bathroom and look at this! You won't believe it!
jokes.setup: Bender excitedly calls everyone to bathroom
jokes.punchline: Implied discovery of human bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Classic bathroom humor setup with character-appropriate robot naivety about human functions.

jokes.text: Bender, it's okay to be proud, but don't be a showoff.
jokes.setup: Bender's bathroom excitement
jokes.punchline: Leela's diplomatic response to inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Leela's diplomatic understatement works as perfect straight-woman response to Bender's crudeness.

jokes.text: You look a lot better than you used to. You're not so bad yourself, big boy.
jokes.setup: Amy compliments human Bender's appearance
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately hits on her
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent moment showing Bender's immediate adaptation to human flirtation behaviors.

jokes.text: Hey, that felt great! Nah, it's not working anymore.
jokes.setup: Bender presumably kissed or touched Amy
jokes.punchline: Immediate assessment that the physical sensation has stopped
jokes.explanation: Bender's robotic approach to human physical sensations creates awkward comedy.

jokes.text: Speak for yourself.
jokes.setup: Bender says physical sensation isn't working anymore
jokes.punchline: Amy implies it's still working for her
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback with sexual implication. Economy is good but the joke is predictable.

jokes.text: You drank and smoked as a robot. But now it's bad for me! Woo!
jokes.setup: Observation about Bender's previous robot habits
jokes.punchline: His excitement about substances now being harmful
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bender's perverse joy in self-destruction now having consequences.

jokes.text: Why didn't anyone tell me tasting things tasted so good?
jokes.setup: Bender tries food as human
jokes.punchline: Redundant phrasing about taste tasting good
jokes.explanation: The redundant phrasing creates a mild wordplay joke while showing Bender's amazement at taste.

jokes.text: What's going on? That rhythm, it's doing something to my human butt!
jokes.setup: Music is playing
jokes.punchline: Bender's confused description of the urge to dance
jokes.explanation: Bender's robot-perspective description of the human impulse to dance is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: I bet I can eat nachos and go to the bathroom at the same time!
jokes.setup: Bender learning about human self-control
jokes.punchline: His idea of multitasking involves gross bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender pushing boundaries with disgusting multitasking. Character-perfect but relies on gross-out.

jokes.text: Goodbye moderation!
jokes.setup: Bender is about to go on week-long bender
jokes.punchline: Formal farewell to self-control
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment with great economy. The formal farewell to moderation is very quotable.

jokes.text: As I've said before, I used reverse fossilization... which is the reverse of regular, uh, eh...
jokes.setup: Professor trying to explain his process to Nobel committee
jokes.punchline: Trails off realizing the circular logic of his explanation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Professor's scattered brilliance and circular reasoning.

jokes.text: Ah! Then without further stalling for time, I present to the Nobel judges...
jokes.setup: Professor was obviously stalling
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges he was stalling while claiming not to stall further
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke acknowledging the obvious stalling creates mild humor through self-awareness.

jokes.text: Pulse, 300. Liver, failing. Cholesterol, 40? Well, that's not so bad. No, I mean 40 pounds!
jokes.setup: Doctor reading concerning medical stats
jokes.punchline: Cholesterol number seems low until revealed to be in pounds
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect timing. The scale revelation is both shocking and absurd.

jokes.text: Come here and give old Bender a kiss.
jokes.setup: Bender is now a grotesquely obese party animal
jokes.punchline: Demands affection from distinguished scientists
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of disgusting Bender propositioning scientists. Character-consistent but basic.

jokes.text: Hey, you like grilled cheese?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to seduce Nobel judges
jokes.punchline: Pathetic pickup line about food
jokes.explanation: The mundane pickup line contrasts with formal setting. Character-appropriate but simple.

jokes.text: Not only do you not deserve a Nobel Prize... for loosing this man-ball upon the world... but you are hereby kicked out of the Academy of Science!
jokes.setup: Judge reacting to grotesque human Bender
jokes.punchline: Describes him as a 'man-ball' and kicks Professor out
jokes.explanation: The term 'man-ball' is a creative description of Bender's spherical state. Solid visual comedy.

jokes.text: Wait! As men of science, are not your minds open to new ideas?
jokes.setup: Professor being kicked out for bad science
jokes.punchline: Appeals to scientific open-mindedness after creating disaster
jokes.explanation: Ironic appeal to scientific principles after unleashing a disaster. Character-appropriate desperation.

jokes.text: Young man, you've opened our minds and swayed our hearts. Let us therefore... Party!
jokes.setup: Formal scientific language suggesting serious consideration
jokes.punchline: Concludes with decision to party
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from formal scientific consideration to party decision. Structure is strong.

jokes.text: He's dead. When did he die? About twelve hours ago when the party started. But he just said 'Woo!' No, that was air escaping from the folds of his fat.
jokes.setup: Bender seemingly responds to Nobel Prize award
jokes.punchline: Revealed he's been dead for hours, sound was just gas
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark reveal that recontextualizes the entire party sequence. The gas explanation is perfectly grotesque.

jokes.text: Woo!
jokes.setup: Previous explanation that 'Woo!' was gas from dead Bender
jokes.punchline: Another 'Woo!' confirms the grotesque explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that confirms the dark humor. Maximum economy with just the sound effect.

jokes.text: Let's get him out of here. He's starting to smell up the joint.
jokes.setup: Emotional eulogy for dead Bender
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to practical concern about smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - shifting from sentiment to practicality. Good contrast with previous line.

jokes.text: Woo! Woo! Woo!
jokes.setup: Previous gas escaping jokes
jokes.punchline: Multiple gas escapes as they move the body
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the gas joke with multiple escapes. Good callback but diminishing returns.

jokes.text: I'm good at video games and bad at everything else. I wish life was like a video game.
jokes.setup: Fry reflecting on his abilities
jokes.punchline: Admits incompetence at everything except video games
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry self-awareness about his limitations. Character-consistent loser mentality.

jokes.text: Can you say that as a question? What if that thing I said?
jokes.setup: Instruction to phrase wish as question for machine
jokes.punchline: Fry's lazy reformulation shows his lack of precision
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Fry's intellectual laziness and imprecision with language.

jokes.text: Wait. I know that monkey! His name is Donkey!
jokes.setup: Seeing Donkey Kong character
jokes.punchline: Misidentifies Kong as just 'Donkey' showing partial game knowledge
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's incomplete game knowledge - recognizes character but gets name wrong. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: Monkeys aren't donkeys. Quit messing with my head!
jokes.setup: Fry's confusion about Donkey Kong
jokes.punchline: Nixon's frustrated logic about animal classification
jokes.explanation: Nixon's exasperated logical response to Fry's video game confusion works well for the character.

jokes.text: Mama Mia! The cruel meatball of war has rolled onto our laps... and ruined our white pants of peace!
jokes.setup: War breaking out with video game villains
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top Italian food metaphors for war
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Italian stereotype with creative food metaphors for war. The 'white pants of peace' is inspired.

jokes.text: I spent all of ninth grade studying them... except the day my eyeballs started to bleed, and in my opinion...
jokes.setup: Fry explaining his expertise with video game enemies
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of eyeball bleeding from excessive gaming
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing extreme dedication to gaming. The casual mention of bleeding eyeballs is perfectly Fry.

jokes.text: Delicious! Just like stale marshmallows!
jokes.setup: Fry eating power pellets in Pac-Man sequence
jokes.punchline: Describes video game food as tasting like stale marshmallows
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about what Pac-Man pellets might actually taste like. Oddly specific and believable.

jokes.text: Hey, watch out! Uh-oh! Oh, my God! He ate Fry! Fry is dead!
jokes.setup: Fry being chased by Pac-Man ghost
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reaction to Fry being 'eaten' in game
jokes.explanation: Standard video game death reaction. The escalation works but it's predictable for the scenario.

jokes.text: It's okay! I had another guy!
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks Fry died
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to having extra lives like video game
jokes.explanation: Perfect video game logic applied to real life. Fry's casual attitude about having spare lives is great.

jokes.text: All right. It's Saturday night. I have no date, a two-liter bottle of Shasta... and my all-Rush mix tape. Let's rock!
jokes.setup: Fry preparing for space battle
jokes.punchline: Describes pathetic Saturday night ritual instead of military preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry characterization - treating cosmic battle like lonely Saturday gaming session. All details are precisely pathetic.

jokes.text: He's a madman! A madman!
jokes.setup: Fry's illogical but effective tactic
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's panicked reaction to inexplicable strategy
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Fry's video game logic. The repetition emphasizes Lrrr's confusion.

jokes.text: No! Amy, tend to the Widow Packman.
jokes.setup: General Packman has just died
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Ms. Pac-Man' as his widow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant video game pun connecting Packman to Ms. Pac-Man. Perfect integration of game reference with story.

jokes.text: One ship is left only! Come on, Fry! Get it! It's moving too fast! I could never get the last one! My brother always got it for me!
jokes.setup: Final enemy ship moving in classic Space Invaders pattern
jokes.punchline: Fry admits he never could beat this part, brother always did it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Fry's limitation and brotherly dynamic. Relatable gaming frustration.

jokes.text: You are defeated! Instead of shooting where I was, you should have shot where I was going!
jokes.setup: Lrrr has won the space battle
jokes.punchline: Explains basic military strategy to defeated humans
jokes.explanation: Good Lrrr moment of condescending military advice. References Wayne Gretzky quote about hockey.

jokes.text: All your base are belong to us.
jokes.setup: Lrrr declaring victory over Earth
jokes.punchline: Famous broken English video game meme
jokes.explanation: Direct reference to famous video game meme. High quotability but limited originality as pure reference.

jokes.text: Quarters! A million allowances worth of quarters! No slugs or tokens!
jokes.setup: Lrrr demands something from conquered Earth
jokes.punchline: Wants arcade quarters instead of typical alien demands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of alien invasion trope. The specificity about 'no slugs or tokens' makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Fork them over! Forget it, you pixelated pirates! We need quarters to do our laundry!
jokes.setup: Lrrr demanding quarters from humans
jokes.punchline: Leela calls them 'pixelated pirates' and cites practical need
jokes.explanation: 'Pixelated pirates' is clever alliteration that references video game graphics. Good escalation of conflict.

jokes.text: But space invaders need to do laundry, too. Look at Donkey Kong. Have you smelled his loincloth?
jokes.setup: Humans refusing to share laundry facilities
jokes.punchline: Lrrr points out space invaders' hygiene needs with specific example
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic about video game character hygiene. The specific focus on Donkey Kong's loincloth is perfect.

jokes.text: Yes. Go away!
jokes.setup: Lrrr asking if anyone has smelled Donkey Kong's loincloth
jokes.punchline: Disgusted confirmation and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. The economy of 'Yes. Go away!' captures complete disgust efficiently.

jokes.text: What if we put our laundry in with yours? Would that be okay?
jokes.setup: Cosmic conflict over quarters and laundry
jokes.punchline: Lrrr suggests practical shared laundry solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist resolution. Mighty space conqueror negotiating laundry sharing is beautifully mundane.

jokes.text: I guess so. Okay, that settles that. But if this cape shrinks, consider your species extinct!
jokes.setup: Reasonable laundry sharing agreement
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's disproportionate threat over potential cape shrinkage
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane laundry concern to species extinction threat. Perfect Lrrr character moment.

jokes.text: Amy. I mean Leela.
jokes.setup: Machine announcing who gets to ask next question
jokes.punchline: Machine/Professor corrects itself
jokes.explanation: Simple mistake/correction gag. Very basic humor without much craft or impact.

jokes.text: As an alien who was abandoned on Earth... I've never really belonged anywhere. Boo-hoo! Ow!
jokes.setup: Leela sharing emotional backstory
jokes.punchline: Bender's mocking 'Boo-hoo!' and Leela's retaliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Bender's insensitivity and Leela's violent response are both in character.

jokes.text: Jeez, apparently the phrase 'Tone it down' doesn't exist on this planet.
jokes.setup: Leela observing over-the-top Wizard of Oz world
jokes.punchline: Dry observation about the excessive nature of everything
jokes.explanation: Good Leela-style dry observation about the garish Wizard of Oz aesthetic. Character-appropriate wit.

jokes.text: Oh, no! We hit someone! Quick, back into the ship! Nice boots.
jokes.setup: Leela concerned about hitting someone
jokes.punchline: Switches from concern to admiring victim's boots
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from guilt to appreciation. The quick shift reveals character priorities perfectly.

jokes.text: Look, everyone! She killed the Man-Witch of the West! A witch? That explains how these boots magically appeared on my feet! No, you stole them. We saw you!
jokes.setup: Munchkins claim magical boot appearance
jokes.punchline: Directly contradict magic explanation with theft accusation
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Wizard of Oz trope. The direct contradiction of magical explanation is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: Well, it's hard to find shoes that fit me.
jokes.setup: Being accused of theft
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact justification for stealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela practicality. References her one large foot while casualizing theft with mundane reasoning.

jokes.text: Who are you people? Haven't I seen you in some copyrighted movie?
jokes.setup: Meeting obvious Wizard of Oz characters
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of copyright issues
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to copyright concerns. Moderately clever but relies on reference humor.

jokes.text: We resemble but are legally distinct from the Lollypop Guild, the Lollypop...
jokes.setup: Leela's copyright question
jokes.punchline: Legal disclaimer about resembling Wizard of Oz characters
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about legal parody requirements. The trailing off suggests many similar disclaimers.

jokes.text: You mean that Yellow Brick Road? The city council re-named it in 1975.
jokes.setup: Reference to Martin Luther King Boulevard
jokes.punchline: Explains Yellow Brick Road was renamed for civil rights leader
jokes.explanation: Clever intersection of fantasy world with real urban development. The specific year adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Those are great shoes! Oh, thank you. Do they come in women's sizes?
jokes.setup: Amy complimenting Leela's stolen ruby slippers
jokes.punchline: Leela asks about women's sizes for her obviously feminine shoes
jokes.explanation: References Leela's masculine presentation despite wearing obviously feminine ruby slippers. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: 'Then, the car honked its own horn!' A talking scarecrow. Want to come with us to see the Professor?
jokes.setup: Fry telling a scary story to scarecrow
jokes.punchline: His idea of scary is a self-honking car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry naivety - his concept of scary is mundane automotive behavior. Very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: He might give you a brain. Hey! That's not nice to say.
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting scarecrow needs brain
jokes.punchline: Fry takes offense on scarecrow's behalf
jokes.explanation: Fry's innocent defense of scarecrow's intelligence is sweet and character-consistent. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Are you off to see the Professor? 'Cause I could use a heart. A human heart. I need to pump a lot of blood out of my basement.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg wanting heart like Tin Man
jokes.punchline: Reveals need for human heart for sinister basement purposes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark subversion of Wizard of Oz trope. The basement blood pumping implication is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: And I'm the other guy. Courage. Not enough of it. Need some from what's-his-name.
jokes.setup: Hermes as Cowardly Lion character
jokes.punchline: Dismissive, low-energy description of quest and Professor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes bureaucratic disinterest even in magical quest. The dismissive 'what's-his-name' is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Fly, my stupids! Fly out and get them! You promised you'd bake monkey cake today! By 'monkey cake,' I meant your ass!
jokes.setup: Mom commanding flying monkeys
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'monkey cake' was euphemism for violence
jokes.explanation: Good Mom character moment with creative euphemism. The escalation from cake to violence is effective.

jokes.text: We've been skipping for hours. I need to pull over and take 'The Wiz.'
jokes.setup: Group traveling down yellow brick road
jokes.punchline: Bender uses 'The Wiz' (Broadway show) as euphemism for urination
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Broadway show title as bathroom euphemism. Character-appropriate crudeness with wordplay.

jokes.text: What, do I smell or something?
jokes.setup: Everyone backing away from Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's oblivious question about his odor
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender obliviousness about his antisocial behavior. Character-consistent but basic.

jokes.text: Here's why I brought you, you twice-baked barf bags! I've always wanted a daughter to love. You want to get adopted, you skank?
jokes.setup: Mom insulting the group aggressively
jokes.punchline: Reveals sentimental desire for daughter, continues insulting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom character moment - combining maternal desire with her signature abuse. Great tonal whiplash.

jokes.text: Live here and be a witch like you? Yeah. As long as I get to hurt people... and not just dance at the equinox.
jokes.setup: Mom offering adoption to Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela accepts but specifies violence requirement over dancing
jokes.explanation: Great Leela moment showing her preference for violence over typical witch activities. Character-perfect priorities.

jokes.text: I'm melting! Oh! Who would have thought a small amount of liquid would ever fall on me?
jokes.setup: Mom melting like Wicked Witch
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic observation about predictable weakness
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the absurdity of the water weakness. Mom's sarcasm is character-appropriate even while dying.

jokes.text: Well, no point letting her go to waste.
jokes.setup: Mom has melted into liquid
jokes.punchline: Leela pragmatically drinks her remains
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark moment showing Leela's practical nature taken to extreme. The casual cannibalism is shocking but character-consistent.

jokes.text: I want to see them! It's nobody! Now, sign that will.
jokes.setup: Professor wanting to see visitors
jokes.punchline: Hermes trying to get will signed while denying visitors
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about Hermes trying to manipulate dying Professor. Perfect character motivations.

jokes.text: Nothing! But if you have extra courage, I'd haul it away for you maybe?
jokes.setup: Asked what he wants from Professor
jokes.punchline: Frames courage need as favor to Professor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes bureaucratic approach to personal needs. Frames magical request as practical service.

jokes.text: After all, who needs courage when you have a gun?
jokes.setup: Professor explaining Hermes doesn't need courage
jokes.punchline: Gun as substitute for courage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about firearms as courage substitute. Good economy and subversive take on self-empowerment.

jokes.text: Now, world. You put your hands up!
jokes.setup: Hermes given gun for courage
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatens entire world
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how gun immediately corrupts Hermes. The global threat is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: This is a stickup! Give me the bag, old man!
jokes.setup: Fry supposedly getting brain from Professor
jokes.punchline: Instead robs Professor
jokes.explanation: Unexpected turn showing Fry's criminal impulses. Mildly subversive but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Here you go, my friend. 5000 Professor Land fun bucks!
jokes.setup: Fry robbing Professor
jokes.punchline: Professor gives fake theme park money
jokes.explanation: 'Professor Land fun bucks' is creative worldbuilding and perfect Professor response to robbery.

jokes.text: Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Realizing he got fake money
jokes.punchline: Simple, defeated reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to failed robbery. Economy is good but no particular creativity.

jokes.text: Nonsense. Click your big honking boots together three times... and wish to go home to Kansas... to live with your dirt-farming, teetotaling aunt and uncle.
jokes.setup: Professor forcing classic Wizard of Oz ending
jokes.punchline: Describes Kansas life as unappealingly as possible
jokes.explanation: 'Big honking boots' and the depressing Kansas description work well. Makes home sound intentionally unappealing.

jokes.text: There's no place like... I want to be a witch!
jokes.setup: Leela starting classic 'no place like home' chant
jokes.punchline: Changes mid-sentence to assert witch preference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from famous phrase to character assertion. Shows Leela's strong will even under magical compulsion.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Help! What's happening? Uh, sorry. I think there's a problem with your upstairs toilet.
jokes.setup: Leela screaming as she's transported away
jokes.punchline: Janitor explains mundane plumbing problem causing the effect
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing magical transport was just toilet overflow. Perfectly mundane explanation for fantasy element.

jokes.text: I was having a wonderful dream. Except you were there. And you. And you. Never mind, she came to.
jokes.setup: Leela describing her dream to assembled friends
jokes.punchline: Realizes dream included everyone present, Professor dismisses her recovery
jokes.explanation: Good reference to classic Wizard of Oz ending. Professor's dismissive attitude is character-appropriate.
